title: Claude's new Cursor killer just dropped
body_text: CLI are great, but they're not the best way to do agentic coding. That's why Anthropic finally put out an updated version of the Cloud Code desktop app, and it looks great. So, you have the Claude chat, Claude Co-work, and Claude Code all in the same app. And it is a huge improvement from the CLI. I bet it probably even uses less RAM, though I haven't checked that yet. There's a lot to love here, and I am very thankful Anthropic's finally woken up to the fact that a slow, laggy CLI might not be the best way to do real agent coding at scale, especially when you're working on multiple things at once. Anthropic is really hyped about this release. It seems like they've been working on it for a long time. I know I've been seeing teasers about it for at least a few months now. So if your company has an AI budget and you and your team haven't spent it yet, you really need to give something like this a shot, it changes how code review works. To be very clear, those 2 million repos running code rabbit right now are not projects that are removing the human from the code review. That's not the point of AI code review. The point of AI code review is to let the humans focus on the things that actually matter like what does the code do? What are the flows? Is this achieving the goal?
